title: Phase 3.3: tag command — Create, list, delete, restore-from-tag
description:
Implement the tag command for naming backup commits.

Subcommands:
- tag <AGENT> <NAME> [COMMIT]: Create tag for backup commit
- tag <AGENT> --list: List all tags for agent
- tag <AGENT> --delete <NAME>: Delete a tag

Tag naming rules:
- Alphanumeric, underscore, hyphen, dot allowed (supports semver)
- Must not start with dash or dot
- Must be unique per agent

Tag resolution:
- Tags resolve to commit hashes for restore
- Restore from tag: casb restore claude v1.0

JSON output formats:
- Create: {agent, name, tag, commit, success, dry_run}
- List: {agent, name, tags: [{tag, commit, date}]}
- Delete: {agent, name, tag, deleted, dry_run}

Success criteria:
- Tag creation validates name format
- Tag creation validates commit reference
- Tag listing shows all tags with commit and date
- Tag deletion requires confirmation (skip with --force)
- Tags work with restore command
- Dry-run shows what would happen
close_reason: Implemented in src/ + tests/e2e.rs. Verified: cargo fmt clean, clippy --all-targets -D warnings clean, 63 lib + 8 e2e tests pass, release binary 2.9 MB. End-to-end with mock data passed: init/list/backup/history/diff/restore/verify/stats/JSON/doctor/completion all working.

title: Phase 1.6: init, version, help commands
description:
Implement the basic CLI commands: init, version, help.

Commands:
- init: Initialize backup location at ~/.agent_settings_backups
  - Create directory if not exists
  - Create README.md explaining backup structure
  - Create default config file at ~/.config/casb/config.toml
  - Create hooks directory structure (pre-backup.d/, post-backup.d/, pre-restore.d/, post-restore.d/)

- version: Show version string (e.g., 'casb version 0.1.0')

- help: Show comprehensive help message with all commands, options, examples

Success criteria:
- init creates all necessary directories and files
- version outputs correct version string
- help shows complete usage information
- All commands support --json output mode
close_reason: Implemented in src/ + tests/e2e.rs. Verified: cargo fmt clean, clippy --all-targets -D warnings clean, 63 lib + 8 e2e tests pass, release binary 2.9 MB. End-to-end with mock data passed: init/list/backup/history/diff/restore/verify/stats/JSON/doctor/completion all working.

title: Phase 1.1: Project scaffold — Cargo.toml, dependencies, src/main.rs with clap CLI skeleton
description:
Set up the Rust project structure with all planned dependencies. This is the foundation everything else builds on.

Dependencies planned (from PLAN.md section 2):
- clap 4 (derive + complete features)
- serde 1 (derive)
- serde_json 1
- toml 0.8
- tracing 0.1 + tracing-subscriber 0.3
- walkdir 2
- ignore 0.4
- tar 0.4
- flate2 1
- chrono 0.4
- dirs 5
- colored 2

Design decisions:
- NO git2 crate — use std::process::Command for git CLI calls
- NO nix crate — use std::process::Command for hook execution
- Binary target: ~5-8MB static binary

Success criteria:
- cargo check passes with zero warnings
- cargo clippy passes with -D warnings
- cargo fmt --check passes
- Binary compiles and runs with --help flag

title: Phase 3.1: history command — Show backup history with tags
description:
Implement the history command showing backup commit history.

Features:
- history <AGENT> [LIMIT]: Show backup history (default 20 commits)
- Per commit: hash, date, relative date, commit message, tags
- Tags shown inline with commit (like git log --decorate)

JSON output format:
{
  "agent": "claude",
  "name": "Claude Code",
  "total": 42,
  "commits": [
    {
      "commit": "abc1234",
      "date": "2026-05-20T10:30:00Z",
      "message": "Backup Claude Code: 2026-05-20 10:30:00 (5 files changed)",
      "tags": ["v1.0", "before-refactor"]
    }
  ]
}

Success criteria:
- Shows commit history correctly
- Tags displayed with commits
- Limit parameter works
- JSON output is valid
- Total commit count accurate

title: Phase 2.1: Git operations via std::process::Command
description:
Implement git operations using std::process::Command (NOT git2 crate).

Required git operations:
- git init --initial-branch=main (with fallback to git init)
- git add -A (stage all changes)
- git commit -m "message" (create commit)
- git log --format="..." (retrieve commit history)
- git tag (create, list, delete tags)
- git fsck --no-dangling (verify repo integrity)
- git diff --quiet HEAD (check for changes)
- git diff --cached --name-only (list staged files)
- git rev-parse --short HEAD (get short commit hash)
- git rev-parse --verify <ref> (validate commit/tag reference)
- git archive <commit> (extract backup for restore preview)
- git checkout <commit> -- . (restore specific commit)

Design:
- All git calls via std::process::Command
- Error handling with context (which git command failed)
- Dry-run support (skip actual git operations)
- Unit tests mocking git CLI responses

Success criteria:
- All git operations work correctly
- Error messages include command and output
- Dry-run mode skips git operations
- Works with git 2.30+
close_reason: Implemented in src/ + tests/e2e.rs. Verified: cargo fmt clean, clippy --all-targets -D warnings clean, 63 lib + 8 e2e tests pass, release binary 2.9 MB. End-to-end with mock data passed: init/list/backup/history/diff/restore/verify/stats/JSON/doctor/completion all working.

title: Phase 3.4: verify command — Backup integrity checks
description:
Implement the verify command for backup integrity checking.

Features:
- verify [AGENTS...]: Verify backup integrity (all if none specified)
- Per-agent checks:
  1. Backup directory exists
  2. Is a valid git repository (git fsck --no-dangling)
  3. HEAD is valid (git rev-parse HEAD)
  4. At least one commit exists
  5. .gitignore file exists
  6. Check for unreachable objects (informational)
- Report issues vs warnings:
  - Issues (error): Not a git repo, fsck failed, invalid HEAD
  - Warnings: No commits, missing .gitignore

JSON output format:
[
  {
    "agent": "claude",
    "name": "Claude Code",
    "status": "ok|warning|error|missing",
    "issues": 0,
    "warnings": 1,
    "details": ["Missing .gitignore file"]
  }
]

Success criteria:
- All integrity checks work correctly
- Issues vs warnings distinguished properly
- JSON output is valid array
- Human-readable output shows clear status
- Summary shows verified/failed/skipped counts
close_reason: Implemented in src/ + tests/e2e.rs. Verified: cargo fmt clean, clippy --all-targets -D warnings clean, 63 lib + 8 e2e tests pass, release binary 2.9 MB. End-to-end with mock data passed: init/list/backup/history/diff/restore/verify/stats/JSON/doctor/completion all working.

title: Phase 1.3: Agent definition system — AgentConfig struct, 19 built-in agents, multi-location support
description:
Implement the agent definition system with multi-location support.

Data structures (from PLAN.md section 3):
- AgentConfig: key, display_name, locations (Vec<AgentLocation>), is_installed, category
- AgentLocation: path (PathBuf), location_type (HomeDir|XdgConfig|XdgData|Custom), required (bool)
- LocationType enum for path resolution

Built-in agents (19 total):
From asb (13): claude, codex, cursor, gemini, cline, amp, aider, opencode, factory, windsurf, plandex, qwencode, amazonq
NEW (6): kiro, continue, copilot, zed, roo, trae

Multi-location examples:
- Claude: ~/.claude/ (HomeDir) + ~/.local/share/claude/ (XdgData) + ~/.claude.json (HomeDir, file)
- OpenCode: ~/.config/opencode/ (XdgConfig) + ~/.local/share/opencode/ (XdgData)

Discovery system:
- Scan ~/. for dot-directories matching known patterns
- Check against existing agents to avoid duplicates
- Support custom agents from config file

Success criteria:
- All 19 agents defined with correct locations
- is_installed computed correctly at runtime
- Multi-location agents resolve all paths
- Discovery finds new agents not in built-in list
- Unit tests for each agent definition

title: Phase 2.5: list command — Show all agents and backup status
description:
Implement the list command showing backup status for all agents.

Features:
- list: Show all agents with status
- Per-agent status:
  - not_installed: Agent source directory doesn't exist
  - no_backup: Agent installed but no backup exists
  - backed_up: Backup exists with git repo
- For backed_up agents, show:
  - Last backup time (relative: '2 hours ago')
  - Last backup commit hash (short)

JSON output format:
[
  {
    "agent": "claude",
    "name": "Claude Code",
    "status": "backed_up|no_backup|not_installed",
    "last_backup": "2026-05-20T10:30:00Z",
    "last_backup_relative": "2 hours ago",
    "commit": "abc1234"
  }
]

Success criteria:
- Shows all 19 built-in agents plus custom agents
- Status computed correctly for each agent
- Last backup time accurate
- JSON output is valid array
- Output sorted by agent name

title: Phase 4.5: Shell completion — bash, zsh, fish generators
description:
Implement shell completion generators for bash, zsh, and fish.

Completion features:
- Command completion: backup, restore, export, import, list, history, diff, verify, tag, stats, discover, schedule, hooks, init, config, doctor, completion, help, version
- Agent name completion: All 19 built-in agents + custom agents
- Dynamic completion for:
  - restore: commit hashes and tags from backup repo
  - tag --delete: existing tag names
  - import: .tar.gz files

Bash completion:
- eval "$(casb completion bash)"
- Uses compgen for completions
- Dynamic commit/tag lookup for restore command

Zsh completion:
- eval "$(casb completion zsh)"
- Uses _arguments for structured completion
- Dynamic commit/tag lookup for restore command

Fish completion:
- casb completion fish | source
- Uses complete -c casb for completions
- File completion for import command

Success criteria:
- Bash completion works with tab
- Zsh completion works with tab
- Fish completion works with tab
- Agent names complete correctly
- Commit hashes complete for restore command
- Tag names complete for tag --delete

title: Test: E2E integration tests with mock agent data
description:
Write E2E integration tests using mock agent data from generate_mock_agents.sh.

Test scenarios:
1. Full backup cycle:
   - casb init → creates backup location
   - casb backup → backs up all mock agents
   - casb list → shows all agents as backed_up
   - casb history claude → shows commit history
   - casb diff claude → shows no changes (just backed up)

2. Restore cycle:
   - Modify mock agent source
   - casb diff claude → shows changes
   - casb restore claude → restores from backup
   - Verify source matches backup

3. Tag workflow:
   - casb backup claude
   - casb tag claude v1.0
   - casb tag claude --list → shows v1.0
   - Modify source
   - casb backup claude
   - casb restore claude v1.0 → restores tagged version

4. Export/Import cycle:
   - casb export claude /tmp/test-export.tar.gz
   - Remove backup
   - casb import /tmp/test-export.tar.gz
   - Verify backup restored

5. Verify cycle:
   - casb verify → all pass
   - Corrupt a backup repo
   - casb verify → detects corruption

6. Doctor command:
   - casb doctor → all checks pass
   - Remove git temporarily
   - casb doctor → detects missing git

Test infrastructure:
- Use /tmp/casb-e2e-test-*/ as isolated test directory
- Use mock agent data from scripts/generate_mock_agents.sh
- Clean up after each test
- Detailed logging for debugging

Success criteria:
- All 6 E2E scenarios pass
- Tests run in isolated temp directories
- Mock data used consistently
- Detailed logs on failure
- Tests complete in < 5 minutes
close_reason: Implemented in src/ + tests/e2e.rs. Verified: cargo fmt clean, clippy --all-targets -D warnings clean, 63 lib + 8 e2e tests pass, release binary 2.9 MB. End-to-end with mock data passed: init/list/backup/history/diff/restore/verify/stats/JSON/doctor/completion all working.

title: Phase 1.4: Output system — Human-readable text + JSON + TOON output modes
description:
Implement the output formatting system supporting three output modes.

Output modes (from PLAN.md section 6):
- text (default): Colored human-readable output using colored crate
- json: Machine-readable JSON output for scripting
- toon: TOON format (requires external toon.sh integration)

Global flags:
- --json: Enable JSON output mode
- --format <FORMAT>: text|json|toon
- --verbose / -v: Show detailed debug output
- --quiet / -q: Suppress non-error output

Design:
- All commands must support all three output modes
- Colors disabled when JSON/TOON mode active
- Structured output types for each command response
- Consistent error format across all modes

Success criteria:
- Text output uses colored formatting
- JSON output is valid and machine-parseable
- TOON output delegates to external tool
- All commands support --json flag
- Unit tests for output formatting
close_reason: Implemented in src/ + tests/e2e.rs. Verified: cargo fmt clean, clippy --all-targets -D warnings clean, 63 lib + 8 e2e tests pass, release binary 2.9 MB. End-to-end with mock data passed: init/list/backup/history/diff/restore/verify/stats/JSON/doctor/completion all working.

title: Phase 3.2: diff command — Show changes since last backup
description:
Implement the diff command showing changes between current source and last backup.

Features:
- diff <AGENT>: Show changes since last backup
- Compare current source directory with backup repo HEAD
- Categorize changes:
  - added: Files only in current source (new files)
  - removed: Files only in backup (deleted from source)
  - modified: Files that differ between source and backup

JSON output format:
{
  "agent": "claude",
  "name": "Claude Code",
  "status": "success|error",
  "has_changes": true,
  "added": ["new_file.json"],
  "removed": ["old_file.txt"],
  "modified": ["settings.json"]
}

Success criteria:
- Accurately detects all three change types
- Excludes .git, .gitignore from comparison
- JSON output is valid
- Human-readable output shows clear change summary
- No changes case handled gracefully

title: Phase 5.2: config command — Manage configuration
description:
Implement the config command for managing configuration.

Subcommands:
- config init: Create default config file
  - Creates ~/.config/casb/config.toml with commented defaults
  - Creates hooks directory structure
  - Does not overwrite existing config

- config show: Display current effective settings
  - Shows config file status (exists or not)
  - Shows effective values (config > env > default)
  - Shows source of each value

Config file format (TOML):
[general]
# backup_root = "~/.agent_settings_backups"
# auto_commit = true
# verbose = false

[backup]
# exclusions = ["*.log", "*.tmp", ...]
# use_rsync = true

[schedule]
# method = "systemd"
# interval = "daily"

Success criteria:
- config init creates valid TOML file
- config init does not overwrite existing file
- config show displays all effective values
- Config file has helpful comments explaining each option
- JSON output supported for both subcommands

title: Phase 3.5: stats command — Backup statistics
description:
Implement the stats command for backup statistics.

Features:
- stats: Show statistics for all agents
- stats <AGENT>: Show detailed stats for one agent

Per-agent stats:
- Total backups (commit count)
- First backup date + relative time
- Last backup date + relative time
- Storage used (human-readable + bytes)
- Average interval between commits (hours)
- Changes per week (calculated from commit history)
- Files tracked (excluding .git)

Aggregate stats (all agents):
- Total backups across all agents
- Total storage used
- Number of agents with backups
- Per-agent summary table

JSON output formats:
- Single agent: {agent, name, total_backups, first_backup, first_backup_ago, last_backup, last_backup_ago, storage, storage_bytes, avg_interval, changes_per_week, files_tracked}
- All agents: {agents: [...], total_backups, total_storage, total_storage_bytes}

Success criteria:
- Statistics computed accurately
- Human-readable output formatted as table
- JSON output is valid
- Changes per week calculation correct
- Storage calculation handles large sizes (KB, MB, GB)

title: Phase 1.2: Config system — TOML config loader, env var overrides, defaults
description:
Implement configuration loading from ~/.config/casb/config.toml with environment variable overrides.

Config structure (from PLAN.md section 7):
- [general]: backup_root, auto_commit, verbose, quiet, output_format
- [backup]: exclusions list, use_rsync, checksum_verify
- [schedule]: method (systemd|cron|none), interval (hourly|daily|weekly)
- [agents.*]: custom agent definitions

Environment variables (override config file):
- CASB_BACKUP_ROOT (default: ~/.agent_settings_backups)
- CASB_AUTO_COMMIT (default: true)
- CASB_VERBOSE (default: false)
- CASB_CONFIG (default: ~/.config/casb/config.toml)

Precedence: config file > environment variable > default

Success criteria:
- Config loads from TOML file
- Environment variables override config values
- Defaults apply when neither config nor env var set
- Invalid config produces clear error messages
- Unit tests for config loading with various scenarios

title: Phase 5.5: Installer script — curl|bash one-liner
description:
Create production-grade curl|bash installer script.

Installer features:
- One-liner: curl -fsSL "..." | bash
- Detects OS (Linux/macOS/Windows via WSL)
- Checks prerequisites: git, Rust toolchain (or downloads prebuilt binary)
- Installs binary to ~/.local/bin/casb
- Sets up shell completion automatically
- Creates default config file
- Idempotent: safe to re-run

Installation steps:
1. Detect platform (OS + architecture)
2. Download prebuilt binary from GitHub releases (or compile from source)
3. Install to ~/.local/bin/casb
4. Run casb init to set up config and backup location
5. Install shell completion for detected shell
6. Verify installation: casb version

Shell completion setup:
- Bash: Add eval "$(casb completion bash)" to ~/.bashrc
- Zsh: Add eval "$(casb completion zsh)" to ~/.zshrc
- Fish: Add casb completion fish | source to config

Success criteria:
- One-liner installs casb correctly
- Binary runs after installation
- Shell completion works
- Config file created
- Idempotent (re-run produces same result)
- Works on Linux, macOS, Windows (WSL)

title: Phase 2.3: backup command — Single agent + all agents, parallel support
description:
Implement the backup command supporting single agent and all agents.

Features:
- backup [AGENTS...]: Backup specified agents (all if none specified)
- Per-agent backup flow:
  1. Check agent is installed (source directory exists)
  2. Run pre-backup hooks
  3. Initialize git repo in backup dir if needed
  4. Sync files (rsync or cp fallback)
  5. Create git commit if auto_commit enabled
  6. Run post-backup hooks
- Parallel backup for multiple agents (when not in JSON mode)
- Summary output: backed_up, skipped, failed counts

JSON output format:
{
  "results": [{agent, name, status, source, destination, commit, changes}],
  "summary": {backed_up, skipped, failed},
  "dry_run": bool
}

Dry-run support:
- Shows what would be backed up
- Lists files that would change (via rsync --itemize-changes)
- Does not create commits

Success criteria:
- Single agent backup works correctly
- All agents backup works with summary
- Skips agents not installed
- Parallel backup completes faster than sequential
- JSON output is valid and complete
- Dry-run shows accurate preview without changes
close_reason: Implemented in src/ + tests/e2e.rs. Verified: cargo fmt clean, clippy --all-targets -D warnings clean, 63 lib + 8 e2e tests pass, release binary 2.9 MB. End-to-end with mock data passed: init/list/backup/history/diff/restore/verify/stats/JSON/doctor/completion all working.

title: Phase 5.1: doctor command — Health check
description:
Implement the doctor command for comprehensive health checking.

Checks performed:
1. git installed and working (git --version)
2. rsync availability (with fallback info if missing)
3. Backup root exists and is writable (~/.agent_settings_backups)
4. Config file valid (~/.config/casb/config.toml)
5. Each installed agent's source directory readable
6. Each backup repo is valid (git fsck)
7. Hooks directories exist
8. Schedule status (cron/systemd)
9. Disk space check (enough space for backups)

Output format:
- Each check shows: ✓ PASS, ⚠ WARNING, or ✗ FAIL
- Summary at end: total checks, passed, warnings, failed
- Actionable recommendations for failures

JSON output:
{
  "checks": [
    {"name": "git_installed", "status": "pass|warn|fail", "detail": "git 2.45.1"},
    {"name": "rsync_available", "status": "pass|warn|fail", "detail": "rsync 3.2.7"},
    ...
  ],
  "summary": {"total": 9, "passed": 7, "warnings": 1, "failed": 1}
}

Success criteria:
- All 9 checks implemented
- Accurate status for each check
- Actionable recommendations for failures
- JSON output valid
- Works even when some checks fail

title: Phase 4.4: discover command — Scan for new AI agents
description:
Implement the discover command for finding new AI coding agents.

Features:
- discover: Interactive mode — prompt for each found agent
- discover --auto: Automatically add all found agents
- discover --list: Just list found agents without adding

Discovery patterns (agents known but not shipped by default):
- .kodu → Kodu
- .continue → Continue
- .sourcegraph → Sourcegraph CLI
- .tabby → Tabby
- .cody → Cody
- .copilot → GitHub Copilot CLI
- .tabnine → Tabnine
- .supermaven → Supermaven
- .aide → Aide
- .roo → Roo Code
- .void → Void Editor
- .zed → Zed Editor
- .trae → Trae
- .melty → Melty

Discovery process:
1. Scan ~/. for dot-directories matching discovery patterns
2. Skip directories already in known agents (built-in + custom)
3. Present new findings to user
4. If adding, save to ~/.config/casb/custom_agents file

Custom agents file format (key=folder):
kiro=.kiro
continue=.continue

JSON output:
{
  "discovered": [{key, folder, name, path}],
  "added": ["kiro", "continue"]
}

Success criteria:
- Discovers agents not in built-in list
- Interactive mode prompts correctly
- Auto mode adds all found agents
- List mode shows without adding
- Custom agents saved to config file
- Custom agents loaded on next run
- JSON output valid

title: Phase 4.3: hooks system — pre/post backup/restore hook execution
description:
Implement the hooks system for running scripts before/after backup and restore operations.

Hook directories (under ~/.config/casb/hooks/):
- pre-backup.d/: Run before any backup
- post-backup.d/: Run after successful backup
- pre-restore.d/: Run before any restore
- post-restore.d/: Run after successful restore

Hook execution rules:
- Scripts run in alphabetical order (e.g., 01-*.sh, 02-*.sh)
- Scripts must be executable
- Pre-hook failure aborts the operation
- Post-hook failure logs a warning but continues

Environment variables passed to hooks:
- CASB_AGENT: Agent name (e.g., 'claude')
- CASB_SOURCE: Source config path (e.g., '/home/user/.claude')
- CASB_BACKUP_DIR: Backup directory path
- CASB_OPERATION: 'backup' or 'restore'
- CASB_COMMIT: Commit hash (post-backup) or restore target

hooks command:
- hooks --list: List configured hooks

JSON output for hooks --list:
{
  "root": "/home/user/.config/casb/hooks",
  "hooks": {
    "pre-backup": ["01-notify.sh"],
    "post-backup": [],
    "pre-restore": [],
    "post-restore": []
  }
}

Success criteria:
- Hooks execute in alphabetical order
- Pre-hook failure aborts operation
- Post-hook failure warns but continues
- Environment variables passed correctly
- hooks --list shows configured hooks
- JSON output valid

title: Phase 2.4: restore command — Preview, confirm, sync from commit or tag
description:
Implement the restore command with preview and confirmation.

Features:
- restore <AGENT> [REF]: Restore agent from backup (REF = commit hash or tag name)
- Preview before restore:
  1. Extract backup commit to temp directory (git archive)
  2. Compare with current source using diff -rq
  3. Show changes: files to be ADDED, REPLACED, DELETED
  4. Warn about files that will be deleted
- Confirmation prompt (skip with --force)
- Sync backup to source directory (rsync or cp fallback)
- Run pre-restore and post-restore hooks

Tag support:
- Resolve tag names to commit hashes
- Restore from tag: casb restore claude v1.0

JSON output format:
{
  "agent": "claude",
  "name": "Claude Code",
  "status": "success|dry_run|no_changes|error",
  "commit": "abc1234",
  "tag": "v1.0",
  "source": "/backup/path",
  "destination": "/source/path"
}

Dry-run support:
- Shows preview without making changes
- Exits after preview

Success criteria:
- Preview accurately shows what will change
- Confirmation prompt works (skip with --force)
- Restore from commit hash works
- Restore from tag name works
- JSON output is valid
- Dry-run shows preview without changes
- Hooks execute correctly

title: Phase 5.4: casbignore per-agent exclusions
description:
Implement per-agent .casbignore file support.

Features:
- Each agent's backup directory can have a .casbignore file
- Format matches .gitignore syntax (uses ignore crate)
- Applied in addition to default exclusions
- Created automatically on first backup with sensible defaults

Default .casbignore content:
# Agent-specific exclusions
# Add patterns here to exclude files from backup
*.log
cache/
Cache/
.cache/
*.sqlite3-wal
*.sqlite3-shm

Implementation:
- Read .casbignore from backup directory root
- Parse using ignore crate's gitignore parser
- Apply patterns during file sync
- Support negation patterns (!pattern)

Success criteria:
- .casbignore files parsed correctly
- Patterns applied during sync
- Negation patterns work
- Default .casbignore created on first backup
- User can customize exclusions per agent

title: Phase 5.3: TOON output support
description:
Implement TOON output format support.

TOON format:
- Compact, token-efficient output format
- Requires external toon.sh integration
- Falls back to JSON if toon.sh not available

Implementation:
- When --format toon specified:
  1. Try to load toon.sh from  or ~/.local/lib/toon.sh
  2. If loaded and toon_encode available, pipe JSON through it
  3. If not available, warn and output JSON instead

Integration points:
- All commands that support --json also support --format toon
- TOON encoding applied to final JSON output

Success criteria:
- TOON output works when toon.sh available
- Graceful fallback to JSON when toon.sh missing
- Warning message when TOON requested but unavailable
- All commands support --format toon

title: Phase 4.1: export/import commands — tar.gz archives with pipe support
description:
Implement export and import commands for portable backup archives.

Export:
- export <AGENT> [FILE]: Export backup as tar.gz archive
- If FILE is '-', output to stdout (pipe support)
- Default filename: {agent}-backup-{YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS}.tar.gz
- Archive contains the agent's backup directory (with git history)
- Shows: file size, commit count

Import:
- import [FILE]: Import backup from archive
- If FILE is '-', read from stdin (pipe support)
- Detects agent folder from archive structure
- Checks for existing backup (conflict detection)
- Prompts for overwrite confirmation (skip with --force)

Pipe use cases:
- Remote transfer: casb export claude - | ssh remote 'casb import -'
- Encryption: casb export claude - | gpg -c > backup.gpg
- Cloud storage: casb export claude - | aws s3 cp - s3://bucket/backup.tar.gz

JSON output formats:
- Export: {agent, name, status, output, size, size_bytes, commits, dry_run}
- Import: {status, destination, agent_folder, dry_run}

Success criteria:
- Export creates valid tar.gz archive
- Stdout pipe mode works
- Import extracts archive correctly
- Stdin pipe mode works
- Conflict detection works for existing backups
- Archive structure validated on import

title: Phase 4.2: schedule command — cron and systemd timer management
description:
Implement the schedule command for automated backup scheduling.

Features:
- schedule --cron [--interval hourly|daily|weekly]: Install cron job
- schedule --systemd [--interval hourly|daily|weekly]: Install systemd user timer
- schedule --status: Show current schedule status
- schedule --remove --cron|--systemd: Remove scheduled backup
- schedule --help: Show schedule help

Cron implementation:
- Uses crontab -l to get existing cron jobs
- Adds line: {cron_expr} {asb_path} backup >> {log_file} 2>&1 # CASB_BACKUP_SCHEDULE
- Removes existing CASB_BACKUP_SCHEDULE line before adding new one
- Log file: ~/.local/state/casb/casb.log

Systemd implementation:
- Creates ~/.config/systemd/user/casb-backup.service
- Creates ~/.config/systemd/user/casb-backup.timer
- Runs systemctl --user daemon-reload, enable --now

Intervals:
- hourly: 0 * * * * (cron) / OnCalendar=hourly (systemd)
- daily: 0 0 * * * (cron) / OnCalendar=daily (systemd)
- weekly: 0 0 * * 0 (cron) / OnCalendar=weekly (systemd)

JSON output for status:
{
  "cron": {"available": true, "installed": true, "line": "..."},
  "systemd": {"available": true, "enabled": true}
}

Success criteria:
- Cron job installs correctly
- Systemd timer installs correctly
- Status shows accurate state
- Remove cleans up completely
- JSON status output valid
- Dry-run shows what would happen

title: Phase 2.2: File sync — rsync with cp fallback, multi-location merge
description:
Implement file synchronization from agent source to backup destination.

Sync strategy:
1. Try rsync -a --delete --checksum with exclusions
2. If rsync fails or unavailable, fall back to cp

Multi-location merge (for Claude, OpenCode):
- Single backup repo per agent
- Flat merge with collision detection
- If two locations have same relative path, suffix with location identifier

Exclusions applied during sync:
- .git, .gitignore (preserve backup's git state)
- *.log, cache/, Cache/, .cache/
- *.sqlite3-wal, *.sqlite3-shm
- Agent-specific exclusions from .casbignore

Dry-run support:
- rsync --dry-run --itemize-changes to show what would change
- cp fallback dry-run lists files that would be copied

Success criteria:
- rsync syncs files correctly with exclusions
- cp fallback produces identical result
- Multi-location agents merge without data loss
- Collision detection prevents overwrites
- Dry-run shows accurate preview
- Execute permissions preserved for hook scripts

title: Phase 1.5: Smart filtering system — Default exclusions, .casbignore support
description:
Implement the file filtering/exclusion system for backups.

Default exclusions (from PLAN.md section 5):
- Logs: *.log, *.tmp, *.temp, *.swp, *~
- OS files: .DS_Store, Thumbs.db
- Caches: **/cache/, **/Cache/, **/.cache/
- SQLite temp files: *.sqlite3-wal, *.sqlite3-shm
- Agent-specific: **/paste-cache/, **/logs_*.sqlite

Key design decisions:
- SQLite databases (*.sqlite) ARE backed up (only WAL/SHM excluded)
- Per-agent .casbignore files supported (like .gitignore)
- Uses ignore crate for .gitignore-style pattern matching

Success criteria:
- Default exclusions applied to all agents
- .casbignore files parsed correctly
- SQLite DBs backed up, WAL/SHM excluded
- Unit tests for exclusion patterns
